title: 3 luxury goods stocks to splurge on in 2024
text: With the luxury goods sector steadily aiming to reclaim its pre-pandemic glory, the trajectory in 2023 has been marked by a resilient rebound. Amid the rebound, the appeal of luxury goods stocks persists, captivating investors in pursuit of blending luxury and financial prosperity.

title: Ralph Lauren Signs Strategic Alliance With Haworth To Expand In The Luxury Furniture Market
text: Ralph Lauren has signed a long-term agreement with the family-owned, Michigan-based Haworth Lifestyle Design group to expand the production and distribution of Ralph Lauren's luxury furniture business.

title: Ralph Lauren (RL) Rises More Than 41% in a Year: Here's Why

title: Last chance to prepare your portfolio for tax season

title: Ralph Lauren Corporation Declares Quarterly Dividend
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Ralph Lauren Corporation (NYSE: RL) announced that its Board of Directors has declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.75 per share on Ralph Lauren Corporation Common Stock. The dividend is payable on January 12, 2024 to shareholders of record at the close of business on December 29, 2023. title: 3 Cyclical Stocks to Avoid as the Economy Slows Down
text: Amid the brewing economic tempest, it's imperative for investors reevaluate their portfolios, particularly with regards to cyclical stocks to sell. With the uncertain U.S. economy, wagering on these stocks becomes a liability.

title: 5 Dividend Growth Stocks To Consider Buying
text: Korn Ferry, Gilead Sciences, Qualcomm, Ralph Lauren, and Manpower are identified as five companies with high dividend yields and strong capacity for future dividend increases. Korn Ferry is a leader in leadership and talent consulting services with significant growth opportunities. Gilead Sciences has pivoted towards new products and acquisitions to improve its outlook, though it still faces headwinds from COVID-related revenue decline.

title: Ralph Lauren Presents First Artist in Residence Collaboration
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) today launched Polo Ralph Lauren x Naiomi Glasses, the inaugural collection of the Company's Artist in Residence program, which invites artisans working with a variety of skillsets and mediums to participate in an immersive collaboration with Ralph Lauren's creative teams. Intentionally focused on the celebration and preservation of heritage craft, the Artist in Residence program is part of the Company's broader efforts to shift from inspiration.

title: Tough Retail Market? That's Not a Problem for This Rock Solid Luxury Icon.
text: Traffic is up at off-price retailers, suggesting consumers are pulling back. Ralph Lauren is leaning into the trend with an increasing focus on outlets.
